title: Encode and Decode Strings
problem_statement: Design an algorithm to encode a list of strings to a single string and decode it back without ambiguity, including strings that may contain delimiters or empty strings.

examples.explanation: Each string is encoded with its length and a delimiter so decoding can reconstruct the exact list.

constraints: Strings can contain any characters, including delimiter symbols
intuition: Prefix each string with its length and a separator so the decoding function can parse the encoded string unambiguously.

approaches.name: Length-Delimited Encoding
approaches.description: Encode each string as length + '#' + string. Decode by parsing the length first, then reading that many characters exactly.
approaches.why_not: Using a single delimiter without length information can fail when input strings themselves contain the delimiter.

interview_qa.q: Why prefix each string with its length?
interview_qa.a: Length prefixes let decoding know exactly how many characters belong to each string, even if the string contains the separator character.
Empty strings are encoded as 0# and decoded correctly because the parser reads the length and then zero characters.
